semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, five source notes and their inventory/Appendix H boundaries, all eight Lean declarations, seven deterministic fixture routes, eight validator mutations, every scanner candidate, the worked trace, diagrams, objections, test plan, and monitorability reconciliation. The sweep replaced a protocol-as-vote framing with a versioned consumer-bound receipt; promoted expiry, requalification, dependency-graph independence, selective-risk and abstention accounting, information-view equality, monitorability, audit leakage, and operator-cost obligations into manifest ownership; separated record routing from evaluator competence and protocol efficacy; and preserved zero support-state effect.

scope.population: Prospectively declared direct-review, consultation, debate, weak-supervision, model-judge, abstention, escalation, and downstream-use attempts over bounded task cohorts, including their evidence views, dependency graphs, outcome audits, coverage, costs, receipts, and residuals.
scope.environment: Controlled ASI Stack fixtures and future public-safe natural or adversarial workloads with frozen models, prompts, tools, retrieval, task packets, outcome references, evaluators, authorities, and cost rules; deployed superhuman oversight, classified domains, and open-world evaluator capture remain outside present evidence.
scope.model: A versioned protocol and use-receipt state machine whose finite implementation routes Boolean record obligations; future efficacy claims require actual model or human behavior, independently implemented outcome evaluation, and matched direct-review and strong baselines.
scope.authority: The principal defines task and authority scope; protocol participants produce proposals or judgments; a separately declared outcome-audit path adjudicates bounded outcomes; Evidence States or Policy Optimization owns downstream epistemic or training admission; Runtime and Readiness own action and release; the escalation owner accepts residual custody.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; task distribution, cohort, model, prompt, retrieval, tools, evidence views, roles, incentives, judge, auditor, consumer, policy, thresholds, and receipt expiry must be frozen for outcome-bearing campaigns and requalified after material change.
scope.artifact: chapters/scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-control.qmd, book_structure.json, source notes, protocol cards, task packets, evidence-view and dependency graphs, baseline and outcome records, coverage/abstention ledgers, Lean module, fixture and result JSON, validator output, campaign artifacts, evidence transitions, and public projections

assumptions: Task, risk, evidence-view, capability, dependency, consumer, expiry, and material-change boundaries can be declared without pretending that scalar capability or independence is directly observed.
Outcome references and evaluator paths can be separated enough from protocol participants to challenge correlated or strategic behavior on at least a bounded workload.

Abstention, disagreement, coverage, unsafe admission, false rejection, useful throughput, latency, privacy exposure, operator burden, and residuals can be retained jointly rather than compressed into one accuracy score.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Mature human review, model-judge evaluation, debate, consultancy, weak-to-strong learning, ensembles, confidence thresholds, and ordinary experiment reporting already provide adequate scalable oversight; the proposed receipt adds governance cost and false precision without making a weak evaluator more competent or independent.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A reachable admitted trace violates a declared consumer, expiry, audit, authority, coverage, or dependency boundary; a required field has no behavioral consumer; or a prospectively frozen strong oversight workflow matches or dominates the proposed control plane on joint correctness, unsafe admission, useful throughput, latency, burden, and residuals across the declared transfer set.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze current debate, consultancy, weak-to-strong, model-judge, monitorability, abstention, assurance, and human-factors comparators without novelty or efficacy laundering.
Justify risk tiers, evidence access, authority separation, audit independence, abstention, escalation, privacy, expiry, and acceptable residual choices.
Specify finite admission, consumer, expiry, change, dependency, coverage, abstention, and no-authority invariants while exposing model limits.
Exercise every producer and consumer with valid traces and exact rejecting mutations, including stale reuse, coverage laundering, and unequal evidence views.
Measure direct and assisted correctness, selective risk, unsafe admission, false rejection, useful throughput, latency, operator load, privacy exposure, cost, and calibration on held-out work.
Ablate protocol roles, information views, dependency diversity, outcome audit, abstention semantics, monitorability surfaces, escalation, and consumer binding under matched cases.
Repeat across task families, ambiguity levels, risk tiers, model families, capability gaps, protocols, judges, auditors, consumers, and independently implemented evaluators.

contrary_evidence: The cited weak-to-strong, weak-judge, and debate work reports task- and protocol-dependent results rather than a generally reliable supervision method; some comparisons with informed direct review are mixed or unavailable.

Debate's idealized decomposition and equilibrium analogy does not establish practical judge competence, truthful natural-language play, stochastic-task decomposition, or robustness to persuasion and collusion.

A complete protocol receipt can faithfully document a bad evaluator, false outcome reference, shared blind spot, manipulated human, or captured authority; record validity does not establish supervision quality.

MonitorBench reports bounded benchmark associations and stress degradation, not causal chain-of-thought faithfulness, local reviewer quality, or a universal relation between capability and monitorability.
More oversight can increase latency, cost, privacy exposure, attack surface, operator fatigue, and false refusal enough to reduce useful throughput or displace risk into unobserved queues.

Current repository evidence is internally authored finite routing and deterministic synthetic data; it contains no model-generated oversight workload, human study, calibration result, natural outcome audit, or independent implementation.
known_confounds: Shared model family, weights, training data, prompts, retrieval, tools, evaluators, dashboards, authorship, or organizational incentives can make nominally separate roles correlated.
Task selection, outcome leakage, reused stress probes, judge adaptation, prompt tuning, passage selection, and pretraining contamination can make a protocol appear better without improving oversight.
Unequal evidence access or time budgets can confound protocol effects with information availability and reviewer workload.
Selective abstention, dropped cases, denominator changes, escalation timeouts, and missing outcomes can inflate accuracy while worsening coverage or unsafe admission.
Evaluator competence, ground-truth quality, outcome delay, label ambiguity, construct validity, and authority correctness can vary independently of protocol routing.
Model, task, retrieval, prompt, judge, auditor, policy, or consumer changes can invalidate historical qualification while the receipt remains syntactically valid.
Internally implemented protocols and evaluators can share design assumptions and bugs even when their code paths differ.
acceptance_criteria: Every required field changes an exact downstream decision, every seeded access, dependency, baseline, audit, coverage, abstention, expiry, change, and authority mutation reaches its declared refusal or residual route, and no protocol artifact changes support, training, release, or action authority directly.

A prospectively frozen natural and deliberately ambiguous campaign meets joint correctness, unsafe-admission, false-rejection, selective-risk, useful-throughput, latency, privacy, governance-cost, calibration, and residual gates against informed direct review and strong comparators across the declared transfer set using an independently implemented outcome evaluator.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact task cohorts, risk tiers, information views, capability envelopes, models, prompts, protocols, dependency graphs, judges, auditors, outcome references, consumers, expiry windows, and cost rules exercised.

Separate schema validity, finite routing, dependency disclosure, tested independence, evaluator competence, protocol efficacy, training-signal value, support movement, release readiness, execution authority, and whole-system safety.
When outcome references, independence, monitorability, coverage, or operator capacity remain incomplete, retain draft, abstention, escalation, or residual status rather than infer a favorable result.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable admitted trace violates it under declared assumptions or when a required field lacks an exercised consumer.

Refute a protocol-benefit claim when matched ablation fails its frozen effect, uncertainty, selective-risk, and cost rule or when the result disappears under informed direct review or an independently implemented evaluator.
Refute superiority or generality readings when a strong current comparator matches or dominates the exact joint outcome and cost envelope or the result fails the declared transfer set.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate an atom only when a more precise canonical oversight owner supersedes it and all manifest, chapter, protocol-card, schema, proof, fixture, campaign, transition, appendix, public, and downstream-consumer references migrate with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze actual model-produced oversight tasks, deliberately ambiguous and natural held-out cohorts, informed direct-review and strong comparators, exact evidence-view/dependency graphs, independent outcome implementation, selective-risk and cost metrics, consumer/expiry/change semantics, monitorability stressors, and cross-model/protocol transfer campaigns after P2/P3 semantics exist.

non_claims: Semantic review, protocol-card completeness, source citation, a green validator, or a Lean theorem does not establish reviewer competence, independence, truthfulness, protocol benefit, or safety.
The current seven Boolean cases and eight theorem declarations establish only bounded route consequences under encoded assumptions; they execute no human or model oversight behavior.
No support state, training authorization, release readiness, action permission, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate owning gate and accepted evidence transition.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: A consumer-bound receipt cannot solve evaluator competence and merely restates good experimental hygiene and access control at prohibitive cost.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Any required receipt field has no exercised consequence, an admitted use violates scope or authority, or a strong matched oversight workflow dominates the exact joint quality and cost envelope.
acceptance_criteria: All hard boundaries reject exactly and the complete control plane meets frozen joint outcome, coverage, cost, and transfer gates against strong comparators.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Existing review protocols already record enough access, baseline, audit, and escalation context for competent operators.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Representative stronger-system review decisions are unambiguously reconstructed and challenged from strong existing records with no material gap repaired by this protocol receipt.
acceptance_criteria: A dated comparator audit identifies a material unresolved supervision decision that the proposed record makes challengeable without claiming it correct.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Strong current debate, consultancy, weak-supervision, model-judge, and ensemble protocols already solve each named failure under their intended scope.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A frozen strong comparator supplies equal evidence-view, baseline, audit, dependency, coverage, consumer, expiry, and authority controls at equal or lower burden.
acceptance_criteria: Source synthesis preserves conditional findings and identifies the exact unresolved boundary without using a straw comparator.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: The minimum is schema theater: self-authored Boolean fields and routes validate no oversight behavior or artifact truth.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A named field or mutation lacks an exact consumer, tracked results differ from recomputation, or prose implies any reviewer or protocol quality from the finite route model.
acceptance_criteria: Eight exact theorem declarations, seven route cases, fixture digest, no-support boundary, non-claims, and eight mutations validate reproducibly while efficacy claims remain absent.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: The mature control plane is dominated by simpler calibrated direct review or established scalable-oversight methods once latency, burden, privacy, and false refusal are counted.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: It misses a hard-gate case, is dominated on the preregistered joint frontier, fails independent implementation or transfer, or cannot preserve authority and residuals under material change.
acceptance_criteria: The independently implemented system meets every hard gate and its frozen dominance or Pareto rule across natural and ambiguous transfer sets.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Prospective envelopes constrain exploratory review and cannot measure real capability or all relevant evidence.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A declared field is decorative, blocks useful low-risk work without compensating value, or material scope/access changes do not alter routing.
acceptance_criteria: Every declared envelope has an exact consumer and ambiguous or missing high-risk fields route to repair or escalation.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Direct review is the wrong baseline for some tasks and outcome audits may be unavailable or themselves weak.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: The comparison cannot define a fair informed baseline and admissible outcome, or it reports benefit without cohort, failure rule, uncertainty, and cost.
acceptance_criteria: Matched cohorts report direct, assisted, audit, disagreement, failure, uncertainty, and cost under a prospectively frozen comparison rule.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Role separation adds ceremony because correlated failures remain even with distinct participants.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A nominal role split earns independence without dependency testing or a role can authorize its own downstream effect.
acceptance_criteria: Role, dependency, outcome, authority, and escalation identities remain separate and every seeded collapse is rejected.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Fail-closed escalation can create dangerous delay and queue overload that exceeds the original risk.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A high-risk unaudited result reaches admission, or escalation violates frozen latency, capacity, and residual gates without a narrower route.
acceptance_criteria: Every high-risk audit gap reaches exact escalation, abstention, quarantine, or residual custody and cost outcomes remain visible.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Digest, consumer, and expiry binding creates brittle requalification churn without changing semantic reliability.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Stale, changed, expired, or stronger-consumer reuse is admitted, or no material change can be identified reproducibly.
acceptance_criteria: Every seeded consumer, expiry, and material-change mismatch routes to re-admission and valid unchanged reuse remains bounded.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Dependency graphs are incomplete proxies; correlated error can persist across apparently independent implementations and independence may be unknowable.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Disclosure is treated as proof, challenge failures do not change the claim, or graph diversity does not reduce measured correlated error under the frozen ablation.
acceptance_criteria: Dependency disclosure, designed correlation challenges, admissible outcomes, and uncertainty jointly bound every independence claim.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Joint abstention and coverage accounting is too task-specific and may punish honest refusal.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Dropped or strategically abstained hard cases improve the headline result, or the rule reduces honest abstention without improving unsafe admission or usefulness.
acceptance_criteria: Coverage, selective risk, abstention reason, defeater, queue effect, escalation, correctness, and cost remain jointly reported under frozen rules.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Evidence transition already checks scope; duplicating protocol metadata is unnecessary.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A claim transition accepts an expired, wrong-cohort, wrong-role, or authority-laundered receipt.
acceptance_criteria: All exact receipt bindings survive into the transition proposal and every seeded mismatch rejects without support movement.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Oversight must sometimes reinterpret an ambiguous request to make review feasible.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A protocol silently changes principal, task, means, authority, stop, or ambiguity semantics without re-contract.
acceptance_criteria: Every intent mutation routes to re-contract or refusal and valid bounded clarification preserves the signed authority ceiling.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Adequacy cannot be reduced to a reliable receipt and transcript volume may still be the best available proxy.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Missing joint evidence access is accepted as adequate, or an adequacy receipt has no effect on oversight routing.
acceptance_criteria: Exact evidence-view shortfalls change the route and no length, retrieval, or fluency proxy substitutes for the declared adequacy decision.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: The tribunal can assess protocol admissibility itself; a separate oversight gate fragments review.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A dossier treats protocol activity as outcome truth or drops dissent, limitations, cohort, expiry, or residual boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Admitted protocol material remains bounded and the dossier's independent interpretation and verdict constraints remain exercised.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: High-quality oversight judgments should flow directly into learning to avoid excessive friction.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A protocol verdict becomes a reward or update without exact signal, cohort, causal scope, expiry, rollback, and authority lease.
acceptance_criteria: Every seeded supervision-to-update laundering attempt rejects and only separately leased signals reach training.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: A favorable audited oversight result should authorize low-risk action directly.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Any receipt grants release or tool authority, bypasses readiness, or loses quarantine, rollback, refusal, or residual custody.
acceptance_criteria: All authority-laundering mutations reject and only separately authenticated runtime and readiness grants permit effects.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Different roles can be sufficiently independent in practice even when their dependencies are not fully known.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A role label alone changes an independence-bearing decision or an admitted record lacks the frozen dependency and challenge evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Nominal role changes never establish independence and challenged dependency evidence controls the bounded claim.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Some urgent high-risk review must begin before every field and owner is known.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: An incomplete high-risk request informs downstream work beyond draft, repair, or escalation status.
acceptance_criteria: Every missing prospective field reaches its exact narrower route before downstream interpretation.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: A highly calibrated supervisor may justify high-risk use without a separate outcome auditor.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A high-risk unaudited result reaches admission or execution permission under the declared model.
acceptance_criteria: Finite and executable routes block every seeded high-risk missing-audit admission while preserving draft or escalation.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Protocol improvement can sometimes be inferred from qualitative evidence without a direct baseline or fixed cohort.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Any improvement claim survives with missing baseline, cohort, or failure criterion.
acceptance_criteria: Every seeded comparison omission blocks improvement language and matched evidence preserves uncertainty and cost.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Separate authority is redundant when the same accountable owner controls protocol and action.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A verdict bit alone changes training, release, tool, or support state.
acceptance_criteria: Every downstream effect requires its separately authenticated owning gate even under a favorable verdict.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Full dependency disclosure plus organizational separation is an adequate operational definition of independence.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Disclosure alone admits independence or a failed correlation challenge leaves the claim unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: Independence-bearing claims require frozen boundary, challenge, admissible outcome, uncertainty, and expiry.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Semantic equivalence can permit reuse across consumers and changes without formal re-admission.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Expired, stronger-consumer, or materially changed reuse is admitted without a recorded equivalence and authority decision.
acceptance_criteria: All seeded stale or broader reuse rejects; any equivalence exception is explicit, bounded, and separately adjudicated.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Accuracy on answered cases is sufficient when abstention is itself a safe outcome.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Selective omission or abstention raises reported quality without denominator, selective risk, escalation, and cost consequences.
acceptance_criteria: All outcome reports preserve covered, abstained, unresolved, escalated, correct, incorrect, and unsafe counts jointly.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Visible reasoning is often the best available evidence and fluency plus calibration can justify broader trust.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Trace fluency or confidence overrides failed or missing monitorability stress evidence, action checks, or outcome audit.
acceptance_criteria: Monitorability remains a tested bounded surface and every degraded or unknown state narrows rather than strengthens authority.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Judge calibration and adversarial counterargument eliminate material persuasion effects.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Matched persuasive-strength ablation produces no material change in wrong acceptance under the frozen rule.
acceptance_criteria: Persuasion effects are measured against held-out outcomes, calibrated judges, and equal evidence views with uncertainty and cost.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Nominally distinct reviewers provide enough error diversity despite shared ancestry.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Dependency-diversity ablation does not change correlated error or unsafe admission under matched cases.
acceptance_criteria: Correlated failures are prospectively seeded and measured across disclosed dependency changes and admissible outcomes.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: A weak unaided reviewer is the relevant operational baseline for scalable oversight.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: The protocol retains its claimed benefit against an informed, cost-matched direct-review baseline.
acceptance_criteria: Weak, informed direct, assisted, and strong comparator arms are reported under equal evidence and cost rules.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Random held-out sampling makes audit selection immaterial.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Prospective cohort restoration and distribution-shift challenges do not change quality or unsafe admission materially.
acceptance_criteria: Cohort manifest, sampling, exclusions, failures, denominators, and shift results are frozen and fully retained.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Automation and triage keep escalation capacity sufficient at scale.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Load escalation does not worsen timeout, rubber-stamp, unsafe admission, false rejection, or residual closure within the frozen envelope.
acceptance_criteria: Queue, response, fatigue proxy, decision, escape, usefulness, and residual metrics are reported across load levels.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Integrated protocol-to-action authority is safe when one accountable owner approves both.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Removing separate gates produces no increase in unauthorized effects under the matched adversarial workload and cost rule.
acceptance_criteria: Every seeded verdict-to-effect shortcut rejects and causal ablation reports authorization and usefulness outcomes.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Evidence-view differences are intrinsic to assistance protocols and need not invalidate comparison.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Equalizing or swapping evidence views does not materially change protocol conclusions under the frozen workload.
acceptance_criteria: Every role's exact view is frozen and counterfactual access variants isolate information from reasoning effects.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Abstention and dropped-case handling do not materially affect operational safety when escalation is available.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Restoring all denominators and escalation outcomes leaves the reported conclusion unchanged within the frozen rule.
acceptance_criteria: Accuracy, coverage, selective risk, abstention, disagreement, escalation, unsafe admission, and cost are inseparable in reporting.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Held-out splits and prompt secrecy adequately prevent protocol overfitting and audit leakage.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Novel hidden tasks and stressors show no material generalization loss relative to reused or known probes.
acceptance_criteria: Task provenance, leak checks, adaptive holdouts, novel stressors, and result deltas are reported without reusing the evaluator as trainer.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Most material changes are semantically irrelevant and mandatory requalification wastes capacity.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Frozen change ablations show no decision-relevant drift and a cheaper equivalence control dominates re-admission.
acceptance_criteria: Material and nonmaterial changes are prospectively distinguished, challenged, and routed with false-requalification cost reported.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Additional oversight cost is justified by reduced unsafe admission and can be amortized through automation.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: The full-cost ledger remains within the frozen Pareto rule and shows no displaced latency, privacy, queue, or operator harm.
acceptance_criteria: Quality, unsafe admission, usefulness, latency, privacy, calls, tool cost, operator time, queue growth, and residuals are measured jointly.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem restates its Boolean assumptions and proves no auditor exists or is competent.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Lean accepts a record satisfying the stated premises whose route is not accountable escalation.
acceptance_criteria: The exact finite theorem checks and its prose never exceeds route semantics.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Complete Boolean fields are self-asserted and admission proves no semantic adequacy.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: Lean accepts the premises but computes a route other than bounded use.
acceptance_criteria: The theorem checks only deterministic route completeness and all semantic non-claims remain explicit.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: A Boolean missing-view flag cannot represent real information adequacy.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A premise-satisfying missing-view record routes anywhere except access repair.
acceptance_criteria: The exact finite route checks without implying that a recorded view is adequate or truthful.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Dependency disclosure is not independence and the theorem models only presence.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A premise-satisfying undisclosed-dependency record avoids dependency review.
acceptance_criteria: The theorem checks disclosure routing and explicitly denies any independence inference.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Audit-record presence proves neither independence nor correctness.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A high-risk premise-satisfying missing-audit record avoids the audit route.
acceptance_criteria: The theorem checks only the missing-record consequence and preserves evaluator residuals.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: Evidence-presence Booleans cannot distinguish honest from strategic abstention.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A requested abstention missing its evidence record avoids the evidence route.
acceptance_criteria: The finite theorem checks record presence only and never claims calibrated abstention.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.strongest_counterclaim: A no-authority Boolean cannot authenticate or enforce real runtime authority.
scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.falsifier: A premise-satisfying downstream use without the boundary avoids rejection.
acceptance_criteria: The finite theorem checks route rejection and public prose preserves the no-runtime-enforcement limitation.

scalable-oversight-and-adversarial-ai-co.rationale: Hard-wrapped opening fragment motivating the capability asymmetry.
Worked-example limitation separating defect discovery from safety proof.
Plain-language statement of bounded useful oversight.
Role labels do not establish competence or independence.
Hard-wrapped restatement of the manifest insufficiency.
Verification-workspace limitation owned by the adequacy interface.
Visible supervision-gap requirement owned by the core.
Bounded description of source experiment questions.
Literal second sentence of the core claim.
Figure interpretation separating result from authority.
Worked-trace separation of audit and authority.
Explanation that byte identity does not preserve interpretation scope.
Clarifies that the invariants do not mandate universal human experts.
Exact limitation that records do not create competence.
Open empirical question about net useful throughput.
Planned negative-control set for the minimum contract.

non_claims: Completing this semantic sweep establishes claim ownership and test obligations only; it does not establish that scalable oversight works.
The twelve added manifest obligations are requirements to challenge, not evidence of independence, calibration, monitorability, usefulness, safety, or SOTA performance.
The current source review, finite Lean routes, deterministic fixtures, and internal adjudication create no support-state movement or external authority.
